Milwaukee’s North Point Water Tower started operating 150 years ago, in the fall of 1874. It houses a standpipe that served as part of an innovative system that brought 16 million gallons of clean water every day from Lake Michigan to 100,000s of homes over the next century.

if you’re a customer of ours, the time it takes for water you flush to circle back to Lake Erie cleanly after treatment varies thanks to a lot of factors in the local and regional system. but the treatment process itself, once sewage reaches our plant, takes just about 24 hours.

For 70+ years, the Line 5 pipeline has run through the Great Lakes, already spilling oil 33 times. Now, Enbridge wants to lock in another century of fossil fuel profits by drilling a tunnel under Lake Michigan. Urge Gov. Whitmer to reject the Line 5 tunnel project!

process the flow instead of it overflowing to the environment.

A Supreme Court case over a technical procedural question could ultimately decide the fate of the Line 5 oil pipeline, which tribal nations warn endangers the Great Lakes, their treaty-protected waters, and their ways of life.
As Line 5 moves forward, four tribes are trying to stop it. They claim the pipeline could contaminate the Great Lakes. Their case is now in front of the Michigan Supreme Court.
If the 41-mile reroute gets federal approval, at least 186 waterways, 101 acres of pristine wetlands, and Lake Superior are at risk. Take action to stop the Line 5 reroute.
